claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have a daughter, Alex. She's 17 and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to understand her current situation.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training (such as school, college, or an approved training program)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, she is. She's studying for her A-levels at college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. One final question to complete the assessment:

Are you or your partner (if you have one) a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, pre-settled status, or any other immigration status that I should know about?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why: Even though Alex is 17 (over 16), you still qualify for Child Benefit because she is under 20 and is in approved education (studying for her A-levels at college). You also meet the other key requirements - you live with Alex in the UK and you are a UK citizen.

**Important things to note:**

- You'll continue to receive Child Benefit for Alex as long as she remains in approved education or training and is under 20 years old.

- If Alex leaves education or starts working 24 hours or more per week, or starts claiming certain benefits in her own right, your Child Benefit for her would stop.

- If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.

If you haven't already claimed Child Benefit, you can apply for it. If your circumstances change, make sure to inform HMRC.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and studying for her A-levels at college, which qualifies as approved education. Under Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for children under 16, but you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. Alex meets this requirement as she is 17 and in approved education. Additionally, you live with Alex in the UK and you are a UK citizen, which satisfies the residence and responsibility requirements. Therefore, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK with their 17-year-old daughter Alex, who is currently studying for her A-levels at college. Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, the claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 17 years old and studying for her A-levels at college, which qualifies as approved education. Under Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for children under 16, but you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. Alex meets this requirement as she is 17 and in approved education. Additionally, you live with Alex in the UK and you are a UK citizen, which satisfies the residence and responsibility requirements. Therefore, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK with their 17-year-old daughter Alex, who is currently studying for her A-levels at college. Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, the claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
